title: English Gilds
summary: The checked-in EGilds XML is a General Corpus copy of the University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative encoding published in 1999 from an Early English Text Society reprint dated 1963. It represents Joshua Toulmin Smith and Lucy Toulmin Smith's edited documentary compilation English Gilds, Lujo Brentano's independent preliminary essay, and Richard Morris's grammatical appendix, while keeping these modern contributions distinct from the many medieval and early-modern records. The encoding retains Lucy Toulmin Smith's August 1892 plate-reprint notice and the three additions that notice identifies, so the 1963 carrier is probably downstream of the corrected 1892 issue; the exact 1963 exemplar has not been inspected. A complete public scan of an uncorrected 1870 copy is cached and proves that the XML omits the 1870 glossarial index and general index on pp. 463–483, as well as the reference-update leaf promised in the 1892 notice. The documentary sources are heterogeneous: Parts I and portions of Part II draw on many 1388–89 Chancery guild returns now grouped in TNA C 47/38–46, while Part III and Appendix I derive from separate Winchester, Worcester, Bristol, Tettenhall, and Bodleian witnesses, sometimes through working transcripts. Exact modern shelfmarks and complete public facsimiles remain unresolved for several of those sources.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for EGilds
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The file is 1,619,654 bytes at the recorded CME submodule commit.
It contains 8 DIV1, 87 DIV2, 97 DIV3, 1,532 P, 72 L, 658 PB, 916 ITEM, 1,127 NOTE1, and 871 FOREIGN elements.
The repository file itself has no independent claim to be a new transcription of any archival witness.

entities.label: University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative encoding of English Gilds
entities.description: The electronic text identified as EGilds, published by the University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative in 1999 and currently displayed in the Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse.
notes: The header says the volume was keyboarded by an entity rendered as 'Chrisian' and met an error threshold of one character in 20,000; that name has not been silently corrected or identified.

The current U-M item-information panel displays London, Oxford University Press, 1870, while the embedded source description says first published 1870, reprinted 1963, and supplies a 1963 copy-specific call number. The immediate-carrier relation follows the embedded source description and preserves the public display date as a discovery-layer mismatch rather than treating it as proof that U-M keyed the 1870 issue.
The header claims that all print material, including front matter, was included, but structural comparison with the 1870 scan shows that the glossarial index and index on pp. 463–483 are absent.
The XML also lacks the new-reference leaf promised by the retained August 1892 notice; FRONT contains only the title page, contents, Lucy Toulmin Smith's introduction, and Brentano's essay.

The language declaration omits Middle English even though Middle English is a principal documentary language, and its Old English, German, and Greek labels chiefly describe quotations or editorial discussion rather than the collection's principal language.

entities.label: English Gilds, EETS Original Series 40, 1963 reprint
entities.description: The printed reprint named as the immediate source in the U-M encoding header. No physical or digital copy was inspected during this research.

bibliographic.citation: Joshua Toulmin Smith, ed., English Gilds, completed by Lucy Toulmin Smith, with a preliminary essay by Lujo Brentano, Early English Text Society, Original Series 40 (London: Oxford University Press for EETS, reprinted 1963).
bibliographic.title: English Gilds
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 40
bibliographic.publisher: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

notes: The XML's retention of the August 1892 correction note and its stated additions supports, but does not by itself prove, descent through the 1892 corrected plate reprint.
The exact physical issue used by Michigan and whether it included the promised reference-update leaf remain unverified.

entities.label: English Gilds, corrected plate reprint, 1892
entities.description: A distinct EETS issue reprinted from the 1870 plates with a small set of added notes and corrections by Lucy Toulmin Smith.

bibliographic.citation: Joshua Toulmin Smith, ed., English Gilds, completed by Lucy Toulmin Smith, with a preliminary essay by Lujo Brentano (London: N. Trübner for the Early English Text Society, 1892), cxcix, 483 pp.
bibliographic.title: English Gilds
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 40
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner for the Early English Text Society

notes: Lucy Toulmin Smith's dated notice says that the original Public Record Office bundles had been entirely rearranged, promises a new-reference leaf and longer corrections at the front, and identifies two additions on p. 348 and one on p. 431.
The Berkeley Law Library catalog independently describes the 1892 issue as reprinted from the 1870 plates with a few notes added.

entities.label: English Gilds, first edition, 1870
entities.description: The first printed edition of the edited documentary compilation, including Lucy Toulmin Smith's introduction and glossary, Lujo Brentano's preliminary essay, the three documentary parts, two appendices, and the general index.

bibliographic.citation: Joshua Toulmin Smith, ed., English Gilds: The Original Ordinances of More than One Hundred Early English Gilds, completed with an introduction and glossary by Lucy Toulmin Smith and with a preliminary essay by Lujo Brentano, Early English Text Society, Original Series 40 (London: N. Trübner, 1870), cxcix, 483 pp.
bibliographic.title: English Gilds
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 40
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner for the Early English Text Society

notes: Joshua Toulmin Smith completed Parts I and II apart from minor press corrections and short notes; Part III was in type but required substantial collation, notes, and abstracts when he died in April 1869.
Lucy Toulmin Smith completed the editorial work, wrote the introduction, compiled the glossary with advice from Richard Morris, and supplied most remaining collation and annotation.
The 1870 copy inspected does not contain the August 1892 notice or the later additions preserved in the XML.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of English Gilds (1870)
entities.description: A complete page-image PDF of the University of Toronto copy of the first edition, together with provider metadata and scan-data inventory.
notes: The scan includes the 1870 title page, preliminaries, pp. 1–483, the glossarial index on pp. 463–478, and the general index on pp. 479–483.
The PDF passes qpdf structural checks and is not encrypted; its provider scan-data file records 700 capture leaves, of which the access PDF contains 696 pages.
The title-page image is at Internet Archive leaf n9 and printed p. 483 is represented at leaf n691.

entities.label: 1388–89 Chancery guild returns, TNA C 47/38–46
alternate_labels: Gild certificates returned under the 12 Richard II inquiry
Former Public Record Office Miscellaneous Rolls bundles CCCVIII–CCCX
entities.description: A modern archival grouping of many separate guild returns, writs, and related records, not a single codex or manuscript work. English Gilds prints all forty-nine English returns selected for Part I and translates or excerpts further material in Part II, but it is not a complete edition of the full current series.

historical_designations: Miscellaneous Rolls, Tower Records, bundle CCCVIII
Miscellaneous Rolls, Tower Records, bundle CCCIX
Miscellaneous Rolls, Tower Records, bundle CCCX
holding.notes: The current aggregate reference is supported by modern scholarship; an item-level concordance from Toulmin Smith's bundle and skin numbers to current references has not been recovered.

physical_description.support: chiefly parchment, with some paper reported
physical_description.extent: many separately sized skins, membranes, and papers
notes: Toulmin Smith described 549 skins or membranes across the three former bundles and emphasized their varied sizes and materials.
That historical count is not asserted as the current item count of C 47/38–46.
The 1892 notice says the former three bundles no longer existed as arranged and required new references.
This entity is explicitly an archival source group and must not be cited as if all printed documents shared one physical witness.

entities.label: Winchester Olde Usages roll
entities.description: The fourteenth-century civic roll used for the Winchester text in Part III. The 1870 edition says Winchester's mayor, council, and town clerk lent the original for collation.

physical_description.extent: two skins sewn together and written on both sides
physical_description.dimensions: approximately 5 ft 2.75 in long and 7.5–8 in wide
notes: The edition reports wear, damage, and passages pointing back to older usage or an older exemplar.
The exact current repository and shelfmark were not established from a current institutional catalog.
A collateral French version formerly at Winchester College is related evidence, not the source used for the printed English text.

entities.label: Worcester ordinances volume formerly titled Ordinances, Edward IV
entities.description: The principal municipal volume used for Part III's Worcester ordinances, one of four original Worcester Corporation volumes lent to the editors.

holding.notes: The 1870 edition documents custody by Worcester Corporation; the current Worcester City Collection is held at The Hive, but the exact modern item reference has not been identified.

notes: The first three quires contain eighty-two ordinances dated 1467; later hands fill the remaining quires, and the printed edition selects rather than reproduces the whole volume.

entities.label: The Maire of Bristowe Is Kalendar
entities.description: Bristol's official town chronicle begun by town clerk Robert Ricart in 1479 and continued by later civic officers. English Gilds prints only its fourth part, The Office of the Mayor of Bristol, after Lucy Toulmin Smith collated the proof against the original.
notes: The 1870 edition numbers paragraphs for convenience and omits the manuscript's few side notes.
No complete public digital facsimile was found; a single institutional illustration is not treated as whole-witness access.

entities.label: Charles Innes Pocock's manuscript volume of Bristol record extracts
entities.description: A nineteenth-century working manuscript supplied to Joshua Toulmin Smith. It mediated Bristol material in Part II and initially mediated The Office of the Mayor of Bristol in Part III before Lucy Toulmin Smith obtained access to the original Ricart Kalendar.
notes: This was not a single-source transcript: the edition also used it for Fullers, Kalenders, and Ringers records in Part II.
Its present location has not been identified.

entities.label: Tettenhall customary copy made from an older original in 1604
entities.description: The older copy that supplied the base text for the Tettenhall customary in Part III. Its heading says it was copied on 22 July 1604 from a copy taken from the original.
notes: Joshua Toulmin Smith received this incomplete older copy through T. W. Cooper.
The wording establishes at least one earlier copying stage; it does not justify calling the 1604 copy the lost original customary.

entities.label: Later Tettenhall customary copy supplied by Mr Neve
entities.description: A fuller later paper copy used to fill and correct the older Tettenhall text in English Gilds.

date_statements.label: paper date evidence
date_statements.notes: The edition reports an 1817 watermark; this dates the paper, not necessarily the textual exemplar.

notes: The copy reported that four separate copies had been made in 1604, including one subscribed by a jury and based on more ancient material.
Its present location has not been identified.

entities.label: Rawlinson manuscript formerly designated Misc. 1370
entities.description: The fuller Norwich Gild of St George ordinance book printed as Appendix I through a copy and collation made by G. Parker.

holding.notes: The edition establishes Bodleian custody and the historical designation; a current shelfmark crosswalk was not found.

notes: The edition reports that trimmed edges slightly damaged the text and that the first page was indistinct.

entities.label: G. Parker's working copy of Rawlinson Misc. 1370
entities.description: The intermediary copy supplied for Appendix I and twice collated against the Bodleian manuscript by G. Parker.

agents.name: G. Parker
notes: Identified in the edition only as Mr G. Parker of Oxford; no fuller authority identity is asserted.

rights.component: The U-M digital collection item as currently presented
rights.basis: The current U-M item states that the materials are in the public domain and applies the DPLA No Copyright–United States statement.
notes: This records the current provider assertion; it is not a worldwide legal conclusion about every underlying archival document or reproduction.

rights.component: The legacy electronic text distribution terms embedded in the XML header
rights.basis: The embedded availability statement permits personal, research, teaching, and linking uses with notice but forbids redistribution by commercial and nonprofit content providers.
notes: The legacy terms conflict in practical effect with the current U-M No Copyright–United States presentation and are preserved as a separate historical rights layer rather than silently reconciled.

rights.component: The 1870 printed edition text and typography
rights.basis: The edition was first published in 1870, and the current U-M presentation of the materials applies No Copyright–United States.
notes: This does not determine rights in newly created scans, catalog descriptions, or repository-supplied archival images outside the United States.

rights.component: The Internet Archive PDF and derivative scan files
rights.basis: The item metadata exposes public downloads but contains no rights or license value for the scan derivatives.
notes: Public downloadability is recorded as access evidence, not converted into a reuse license.

rights.basis: The repository README licenses code under GPL-3.0 and non-public-domain output under CC BY-SA 4.0, but that general statement does not clearly establish authority to license the upstream U-M transcription or supersede its embedded legacy terms.

rights.component: New photographs, scans, or supplied reproductions of the archival records
rights.basis: No blanket open license for complete reproductions of the source group was identified; TNA access and copying procedures apply to supplied images.
notes: The age of the underlying documents does not by itself settle reuse conditions for a newly supplied reproduction.

rights.component: New photographs, scans, or supplied reproductions of Ricart's Kalendar
rights.basis: The exact catalog record and contact route are public, but no blanket open license for complete manuscript images was found.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Large, heterogeneous archival groups of guild returns and municipal records
editorial_practices.representation: An arranged documentary compilation in three main parts with selected appendices
editorial_practices.description: The editors selected records to illustrate English guild organization and municipal relations; they did not publish every document found in the former Public Record Office bundles or every page of the local source volumes.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Inconsistent manuscript strokes and stops in the guild returns
editorial_practices.representation: Modernized punctuation and hyphenation intended to clarify sense
editorial_practices.description: Joshua Toulmin Smith explicitly replaced the returns' inconsistent punctuation practice with editorial punctuation, hyphens, and other modern reading aids.
editorial_practices.scope: Part I and other edited documentary passages where the stated practice applies
notes: This is an editorial reading intervention and should not be treated as diplomatic punctuation evidence from the manuscripts.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Part III proofs and Appendix I left incomplete or mediated by working copies
editorial_practices.representation: Corrected texts, notes, marginal abstracts, and readings checked against originals or delegated collations
editorial_practices.description: Lucy Toulmin Smith completed most remaining collation and apparatus; she personally checked the Bristol text against Ricart's original, while G. Parker copied and twice collated the Norwich appendix.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The 1963 reprint's complete bound contents, including the glossary, index, and a correction-reference leaf expected from the 1892 issue
editorial_practices.representation: Encoded title page, contents, introduction, essay, Parts I–III, and Appendices I–II through printed p. 462
editorial_practices.description: Despite a header claim that all material was included, the digital text stops after the grammatical notes and omits the glossarial index and general index on pp. 463–483; it also does not encode the new-reference leaf promised by the retained 1892 notice.

evidence.locator: HEADER; SOURCEDESC; EDITORIALDECL; FRONT; BODY; BACK
evidence.summary: Primary local evidence for the EGilds identifier, U-M publication date and source statement, the 1963 reprint attribution, embedded legacy access terms, encoding practices, edition text, retained 1892 corrections, and source notes.

evidence.citation: Computational inspection of General Corpus EGilds.xml against the checked-in file.
evidence.locator: Element counts; FRONT/BODY/BACK direct-child inventory; printed-page PB ranges
evidence.summary: Confirms 8 DIV1, 87 DIV2, 97 DIV3, 1,532 P, 72 L, 658 PB, 916 ITEM, 1,127 NOTE1, and 871 FOREIGN elements; shows that BACK ends with Appendix II at p. 461/462 and contains no encoded glossary or index despite the contents listing them at pp. 463 and 479.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, English Gilds (EGilds).
evidence.locator: Item header, bibliographic information, table of contents, Rights/Permissions
evidence.summary: Confirms the current institutional item, stable EGilds identifier, public digital-text access, and current Public Domain / No Copyright–United States provider statement.

evidence.citation: Joshua Toulmin Smith, ed., English Gilds, completed by Lucy Toulmin Smith, with essay by Lujo Brentano, EETS Original Series 40 (London: N. Trübner, 1870), University of Toronto copy digitized by Internet Archive.
evidence.locator: Title page; contents pp. v–x; introduction pp. xi–xlviii; essay pp. xlix–cxcix; text pp. 1–462; glossarial index pp. 463–478; index pp. 479–483
evidence.summary: Complete direct book evidence for the 1870 edition's title, responsibilities, imprint, full contents, pagination, editorial statements, historical source descriptions, and the glossary/index omitted from the XML. The inspected copy lacks the 1892 correction notice and additions.

evidence.citation: Lucy Toulmin Smith, Introduction, in English Gilds (1870), pp. xi–xlviii.
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for the state of the edition at Joshua Toulmin Smith's death, Lucy Toulmin Smith's completion and glossary, Richard Morris's contribution, the selective purpose of the volume, and the former Public Record Office bundle structure.

evidence.citation: Source and editorial notes in English Gilds (1870).
evidence.locator: Part I editor's introduction pp. 1–2; Part II source notes; Part III pp. 347–442; Appendix I pp. 443–460
evidence.summary: Primary edition evidence for selection and punctuation policy; the Winchester roll; four Worcester volumes and the 54-leaf ordinance book; Pocock's Bristol extracts and Lucy's Ricart collation; the two Tettenhall copies; and Parker's mediated copy and collation of Rawlinson Misc. 1370.

evidence.citation: Lucy Toulmin Smith, dated August 1892 correction notice retained in EGilds.xml.
evidence.locator: End of FRONT/DIV1[@TYPE='Introduction']; Part III additions at pp. 348 and 431
evidence.summary: Direct evidence that the former guild-return bundles had been rearranged, that a new-reference and correction leaf was intended at the beginning of the 1892 reprint, and that the plate text received two additions on p. 348 and one on p. 431.

evidence.citation: UC Berkeley Law Library catalog record 268555, English gilds (1892), OCLC 13747087.
evidence.locator: Imprint, physical description, series note, reproduction note
evidence.summary: Current institutional catalog evidence for the distinct London 1892 N. Trübner issue, cxcix plus 483 pages, described as reprinted from the 1870 plates with a few notes added; the linked electronic reproduction is subscription-restricted.

evidence.locator: metadata; files entries for PDF and scandata
evidence.summary: Confirms item identifier, 1870 date and creators, University of Toronto contribution and sponsorship, PDF derivative, and absence of populated rights or license fields.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive scan data for englishgildsorig00smit.

evidence.summary: Provider inventory supporting the scan's complete page sequence, title-page and terminal-page deep links, and the presence of the glossarial and general indexes.

evidence.citation: Gervase Rosser Jones, 'English Religious Brotherhoods and Medieval Lay Piety: The Inquiry of 1388–89,' The Historian 36 (1974): 646–659.
evidence.locator: Archival references and discussion of the 1388–89 returns
evidence.summary: Reliable scholarship crosswalking the inquiry's surviving certificates to Chancery Miscellanea C 47, including C 47/38–46, and clarifying that the inquiry produced many distinct returns rather than one manuscript.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, Discovery catalog and record-copying guidance.
evidence.locator: Discovery ordering options; record copying guidance
evidence.summary: Current institutional evidence for searching exact TNA references, ordering open records for onsite consultation, and requesting paid copies; it does not provide a complete public facsimile of the guild-return group.

evidence.summary: Current institutional catalog evidence for the exact Ricart Kalendar reference CC/2/7, Bristol Archives custody, and repository contact route.

evidence.citation: University of Bristol Department of Historical Studies, Robert Ricart's Kalendar of Bristol.
evidence.summary: Institutional scholarly description identifying legacy reference 04720, Robert Ricart's 1479 beginning and work through 1506, later civic continuations, and the manuscript's status as Bristol's official chronicle.

evidence.citation: Worcestershire Archive and Archaeology Service, Worcester City Collection.
evidence.locator: Collection description, catalog availability, extent, and access notes
evidence.summary: Current institutional evidence that the Worcester City Archive is held and fully cataloged by Worcestershire Archive and Archaeology Service, with an online catalog and limited restrictions for fragile records; it does not identify the exact ordinance volume.

evidence.citation: Targeted searches of current institutional catalogs and public web resources for the Winchester roll, Worcester ordinance volume, Tettenhall copies, and Rawlinson Misc. 1370.
evidence.locator: Exact historical titles, custodians, and shelfmark variants searched on 2026-07-14
evidence.summary: No complete public facsimile was found for any of these witnesses; exact current records were recovered only for Bristol CC/2/7 and the aggregate Worcester City Collection, while the other shelfmark crosswalks remain unresolved.
notes: A negative public-web finding does not establish loss, destruction, or impossibility of mediated access.

evidence.citation: General Corpus README.org and checked-in GPL-3.0 and CC BY-SA 4.0 license texts at repository commit 7b67eea3d9b9f7c1a99c6d21d845ac5809f70876.
evidence.summary: Records the repository's general licensing statements while leaving unresolved whether they can license or supersede rights and provider terms in the upstream-derived EGilds XML.

open_questions.question: Which exact 1963 physical issue did Michigan encode, and did that copy descend directly from the 1892 corrected plates and contain the promised reference-update leaf?
next_steps: Inspect University of Michigan call number 820.6 E1 no.40 1963 or obtain full images of its front matter and pp. 348 and 431.
Compare the 1963 copy against both the cached 1870 scan and a verified 1892 copy.
Record whether the missing reference leaf was absent from the 1963 carrier or omitted only during encoding.

open_questions.question: How do Toulmin Smith's obsolete bundle, skin, and membrane labels map item by item to current TNA references within C 47/38–46?
next_steps: Obtain current piece and item lists for C 47/38–46 from The National Archives.
Crosswalk every Part I and relevant Part II source note by place, incipit, language, material, and former number.
Preserve many-to-many mappings rather than forcing one printed unit to one modern item.

open_questions.question: What are the exact current shelfmarks, survival states, and consultation routes for the Winchester roll, Worcester ordinance volume, both Tettenhall copies, and Bodleian Rawlinson Misc. 1370?
next_steps: Send repository enquiries quoting the edition's physical descriptions and historical custodians.
Ask Bodleian staff for a Rawlinson Miscellaneous old-to-current shelfmark crosswalk.
Ask Worcestershire Archive and Archaeology Service to identify the 54-leaf six-quire volume historically bound as Ordinances, Edward IV.
Trace Winchester and Tettenhall provenance without labeling unlocated witnesses lost.

open_questions.question: Which exact archival witnesses underlie each translated, excerpted, or English document in Part II beyond the 1388–89 Chancery returns?
next_steps: Extract every Part II source note and build an item-level source inventory by town and document.

Verify current institutional catalog records for the York, Beverley, Hull, Lancaster, Chesterfield, Lincoln, Stamford, Ludlow, Worcester, Stratford, Coventry, Birmingham, Cambridge, Bristol, Reading, Exeter, and Berwick material.
Model working copies and printed intermediaries separately wherever the editor did not use the original.

open_questions.question: How should the XML's legacy redistribution prohibition be reconciled operationally with the current U-M No Copyright–United States statement and the repository's general CC BY-SA output policy?
next_steps: Request clarification from the University of Michigan Library about the status of legacy availability statements embedded in downloadable CME texts.
Do not treat the repository's general output license as automatically relicensing upstream transcription content.
Keep copyright, provider terms, and repository policy as separate assertions until clarified.

review_notes: The 1870 compilation, the 1892 corrected plate reprint, the 1963 carrier, the U-M encoding, the repository XML, the scan, and the archival witnesses are modeled as distinct entities.
The 1963-to-1892 relation is supported at medium confidence by the retained 1892 note and additions; it is not upgraded to confirmed without inspecting the 1963 volume.
Part I and Part II source statements do not justify treating TNA C 47/38–46 as one physical manuscript or the printed selection as a complete archival census.
Historical shelfmarks and custodians are retained without inventing current catalog identifiers.
The cache contains only the complete public 1870 edition and its provider metadata/scan data; no fragmentary manuscript image was substituted for a complete witness facsimile.
